ground_truth.chief_complaint: Headache, upper abdominal pain and visual disturbances at 28 weeks gestation
relevant_history: Pregnant, 28 weeks gestation (patient context, confirmed by doctor)
Spanish-speaking; consultation conducted via hospital interpreter

vitals.status: blood pressure not yet measured during the conversation

diagnosis.primary: Pre-eclampsia (suspected) - named by the doctor as 'a condition called pre-eclampsia'

procedures: Listen to the baby's heartbeat (stated turn 7)
follow_up: Confirmed patient consent to the planned checks (turns 8-9)
patient_education: Doctor named and briefly explained pre-eclampsia (turn 5)
Reassurance given about taking care of mother and baby (turns 5, 7)

clinical_note.subjective: 31F at 28 weeks gestation, Spanish-speaking (interpreter present). Reports headache since yesterday, upper-abdominal pain, lights in both eyes, and 2 days of hand swelling. Anxious about the baby.
clinical_note.objective: No vitals recorded during the conversation. Blood pressure, urine test and fetal heart auscultation planned.
clinical_note.assessment: Suspected pre-eclampsia (doctor's stated concern) given headache, visual symptoms, epigastric pain and hand swelling in pregnancy.
clinical_note.plan: Measure blood pressure, test urine, and listen to the baby's heartbeat now. Reassure and explain. Consent obtained via interpreter.
